title: Halo: Lost Spartan
description: You are thrown into a post-apocalyptic Halo facility overrun by parasitic creatures. The world pushes you to your limits as you fight to survive against the relentless onslaught of the parasite in this intense survival horror experience.
body:
# **Welcome Spartan to Halo: Lost Spartan**

You're dropped onto the fractured remains of a post-apocalyptic world, a world overrun by a parasitic menace focused on consuming what's left of humanity. The very ground beneath you crumbles into the void.
This Modpack delivers an unforgiving, intense survival-horror experience within Minecraft, capturing the dark atmosphere of the Halo universe.



## About this modpack

**Experience a unique blend of sci-fi horror and hardcore survival**:

### ⚡ The ODST Experience:
**An immersive, atmospheric UI overhaul with custom soundscapes and a Spartan-HUD.**

### 💀 The Flood Menace:
**Encounter brutal, scaling difficulty (Majrusz's Progressive Difficulty) and horrifying Mutant Zombies, inspired by the relentless challenges of the Flood parasite.**

### 🔩 Scavenge & Survive:
**Extreme resource scarcity (configured world settings) and a new hydration/thirst system force tactical gameplay and desperate scavenging.**

**The world will push you to your limits as you fight to survive against these relentless threats. Can you escape the ring?**



### Requirements & Performance
- Minecraft Version: 1.20.1 (Forge)
- Recommended RAM: At least 6-8 GB of RAM  allocated.
- Shaders: We recommend using dark, atmospheric shaders for the best experience.


   
**Please Read (Important for the post-apocalyptic world!)**

**To get the full “Lost Cities” experience, you must manually set the world generation**:

1. Start by creating a new world.
2. Set the World Type to Large Biomes (recommended).
3. Go to the More tab and click the Cities button.
4. Click the Profile button several times until you select rarecities. (You can use other profiles too, but this one is recommended!)
5. Click the Customize button once. Then, click the setting that says standard several times until you see pomkotsmechs:pomkotsworld.
6. Set the Radius to 300. (Recommended: sets the max radius of a city to 300 blocks.)
7. Click the Cities button.
8. Adjust the building generation settings to your liking. (Check the Lost Cities documentation for more detail!)
    
    Reference Links

    Lost Cities official usage：https://www.mcjty.eu/docs/mods/lost-cities/

## Disclaimer!
**This is a fan-made Minecraft modpack inspired by the Halo franchise from Microsoft/343 Industries. It is not an official product and is not endorsed by Microsoft.**
